title: Baalvion for Logistics
excerpt: Control towers, route optimisation, and automated customs — end-to-end visibility and lower cost across global corridors.

seo.title: Logistics & Supply Chain Technology | Baalvion
seo.description: Baalvion builds logistics platforms: real-time control towers, multi-leg route optimisation, and automated customs clearance across global corridors.

challenges: No single view of in-flight shipments
Manual, fragile customs filing across corridors
Delays discovered too late to act
solutions: A real-time control tower with end-to-end visibility
Multi-leg route optimisation on a lane graph

blocks.text: Logistics is a coordination problem at planetary scale: shipments cross many carriers, corridors, and customs regimes, and a single blind spot cascades into delays and cost. Baalvion brings real-time visibility and optimisation to this, exactly as in [a logistics control tower for global corridors](/case-studies/logistics-control-tower).

items: **Visibility:** no single view of where shipments actually are.
**Routing:** decided by habit rather than analysis, so cost and time suffer.
**Customs:** manual filing across incompatible national systems.
**Reactivity:** delays surface too late to do anything about them.

blocks.text: We build control towers with event-driven milestone tracking for end-to-end visibility, multi-leg route optimisation over a lane graph, and connector-based [automated customs clearance](/case-studies/automated-customs-clearance) to ICEGATE, ACE, and CDS — with retries and failover so a shipment never silently stalls.

Logistics engagements draw on [automation](/services/automation), [cloud solutions](/services/cloud-solutions), and [enterprise software](/services/enterprise-software), with [AI solutions](/services/ai-solutions) for forecasting and optimisation.
Operators gain end-to-end shipment visibility, lower route cost through optimisation, automated clearance across corridors, and the ability to spot and reroute around delays in real time.

faqs.q: What is a logistics control tower?
faqs.a: A real-time, unified view of every in-flight shipment, fed by event-driven milestones, with optimisation and alerting — see [our control tower case study](/case-studies/logistics-control-tower).

faqs.q: How does route optimisation reduce cost?
faqs.a: By modelling the corridor network as a lane graph and searching it for the cheapest, fastest, or balanced route using live carrier rates and transit times.
Yes — via connectors to national systems (ICEGATE, ACE, CDS) with async pipelines and automatic failure recovery. See [automated customs clearance](/case-studies/automated-customs-clearance).
